architecture.uml:
left to right direction actor "Non-technical author" as Author actor "Professional author" as Prof node "Personal laptop" { artifact "Generated reports" as html_doc
component "Integrated development environment (IDE)" as IDE
database "Filesystem" { artifact "mbse-lite model" as model
}
} cloud "Public PlantUML server" as PublicPlantUML
model - IDE - html_doc - PublicPlantUML - Filesystem -down-
Author ...> : edits Prof ...> Author ...> : reviews html_doc ...> : renders

description.brief: Self-hosted editing environment for open-source projects

architecture.description:
A personal editing workflow lets a non-technical author maintain MBSE-lite XML models locally and run make occasionally to preview generated reports.
Note: Open source projects only.

interface.description: The author edits XML system models with a plain text editor on a personal laptop.
interface.mechanical: Editing is keyboard oriented, not mouse oriented, for source version control (VCS) purposes.
interface.software: The interface SHALL preserve plain XML files without requiring an MBSE-specific editor application.

interface.description: (Optional) The author may use an integrated development environment (IDE) for navigation, search, and XML editing without changing the model format.
interface.software: The interface SHALL expose the same repository files used by vanilla text editors.
The interface SHALL have a file browser for quick navigation of all nested folders and XML files.

interface.description: A web service that converts PlantUML code to images.
interface.software: The interface SHALL send PlantUML text over a RESTful web interface and receive rendered figures.
The interface SHALL encode / escape the PlantUML code in Base64 encoding [[RFC4648]].
The interface SHALL NOT encrypt the incoming or outgoing data. It is the end user's responsibility to edit / review open source designs only on personal laptops.

interface.description: A document viewer that also supports static figure rendering, hyperlinks, and limited forms of dynamic scripts.
interface.software: The interface SHALL support vector drawings, likely [[SVG]].

interface.description: An interactive interface to invoke product requirement report generation on demand. Likely used by Linux `inotify` daemon to detect file changes.
